title: Kendall
content:
{{Top|fa|leg|italics=1}} {{IndividualShip |image= |name=''Kendall'' |designer= |manufacturer=[[Olanji/Charubah]]According to {{CSWECite|II|407|Olanji/Charubah}}, [[Olanji/Charubah]] manufactured the [[Hapan Battle Dragon]]. |line= |model=[[Hapan Battle Dragon]]''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]'' |type= |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length=500 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]{{FactFile|90|HAP 1-2|Hapan Battle Dragon}} states a [[Hapan Battle Dragon]]'s length as 500 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]. |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine=[[Engine/Legends|Equipped]] |hyperdrive=[[Hyperdrive/Legends|Equipped]] |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament= |complement= |bays=Capture [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport=[[Life support/Legends|Equipped]] |communications= |othersystems= |role=[[Flagship/Legends|Flagship]] |commission= |destroyed= |retired= |battles=*[[Battle of Qoribu]] *[[Battle of Hapes (Second Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Hapes]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Tempest]]'' |affiliation=*[[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]] **[[House AlGray]] |navy=*[[Hapan Royal Navy]] **[[Fleet of the Glorious Defender Queen]] *[[Heritage Fleet]] |fleet= |owners= |captains=*[[Dukat]] [[Aleson Gray]] {{C|Battle of [[Qoribu]]}} *[[Ducha]] [[AlGray]] {{C|Battle of [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]]}} |crewmembers=[[Lalu Morwan]] |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|This is Hapes Battle Dragon ''Kendall'' hailing Galactic Alliance transport ''Longshot''. Heave to and prepare for temporary impoundment.|The ''Kendall''{{'s}} crew attempts to impound the ''Millennium Falcon'', operating under the alias ''Longshot''|}} The '''''Kendall''''' was a [[Hapan Battle Dragon]] owned by [[House AlGray]] that was serving with the [[Hapan Royal Navy]] as of [[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the ''Kendall'' served as the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of the [[Hapan]] [[Fleet of the Glorious Defender Queen]], which was dispatched to aid the [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]] of [[the Colony]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. In doing so, the ''Kendall'' briefly [[Battle of Qoribu|went up against]] the [[Chiss Expansionary Defense Force|forces]] of the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]] in the [[Gyuel system]] before helping to relocate the Killiks in order to maintain peace between them and the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]]. [[40 ABY/Legends|Five years later]], the ''Kendall'' became the flagship of the [[Heritage Fleet]], whose purpose was to aid [[Ducha]] [[AlGray]] and the other Hapan members of the [[Heritage Council]] in overthrowing the current Hapan [[Monarchy/Legends|monarch]], [[Queen Mother]] [[Tenel Ka Djo]]. However, their plot ultimately failed, and the ''Kendall'' was [[Battle of Hapes (Second Galactic Civil War)|pummeled]] by a quarter of the Hapan Royal Navy at the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]]. ==Description== {{Quote|There's no way to transport the Qoribu nests. They are too large.''"
"''Really? So large they couldn't be temporarily rebuilt in the hangars and launching bays of, say, a few Hapan Battle Dragons?|[[Prime Unu]] [[Raynar Thul|UnuThul]] and Han Solo discuss the relocation of the Killik nests|}} The ''Kendall'' was a 500-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long [[Hapan Battle Dragon]] produced by [[Olanji/Charubah]]. Equipped with both a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] and [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight drives]], the [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] possessed launching bays and [[Hangar/Legends|hangars]]—including one designated for captured [[Starship/Legends|starships]]—and a [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]]-walled hold that featured a single observation bubble. The ''Kendall'' also had departure alarms, which were used to announce an imminent jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], as well as [[Intercom/Legends|intercoms]] and [[Life support/Legends|life support]]. ==History== ===Killik troubles=== {{Quote|Battle Dragon ''Kendall''. This is Leia Organa Solo aboard the ''Millennium Falcon''.''"
"Millennium Falcon''?''"
"''Yes. Sorry for the confusion, but we usually travel incognito. I'm sure you understand.|Leia Organa reveals her freighter's true name to the ''Kendall'' and its crew|}} [[File:HapanBattleDragon-SotGSE.png|thumb|left|200px|The ''Kendall'' was an Olanji/Charubah Battle Dragon.]] The ''Kendall'' was serving with the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium's]] [[Hapan Royal Navy|Royal Navy]] and had been given to [[Dukat]] [[Aleson Gray]] of [[House AlGray]]{{CSWECite|I|361|Gray, Aleson}} of the [[Relephon Moons]] to command by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Dark Nest Crisis]]. Involving the [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]] of [[the Colony]]; the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]]; and the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] and its [[New Jedi Order]], the crisis took place in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] and eventually led to the [[Swarm War]]. With the Colony encroaching on [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]] in the [[Gyuel system]] and threatening to escalate the conflict, [[Hapan]] [[Queen Mother]] [[Tenel Ka Djo]] dispatched the [[Fleet of the Glorious Defender Queen]] with the ''Kendall'' as its [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] to aid the Killiks and dissuade the Chiss from immediately assaulting the Killiks. While the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] was on its way to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Qoribu]] in the Gyuel [[Star system/Legends|system]], the ''Kendall'' encountered the Galactic Alliance [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], which was operating under the alias ''Longshot'', and was also on its way to the same system. The ''Kendall''{{'s}} Hapan crew members initially wished to impound the [[Light freighter/Legends|freighter]] but soon discovered its true identity and that it was carrying [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], the [[Parent/Legends|parents]] of the [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] Mother's new [[Consort/Legends|consort]], [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], so Gray let them continue on their way. The Hapan forces under Gray did not immediately engage those of the Chiss when the former arrived in the Gyuel system, but the [[Solo family/Legends|Solos]] soon revealed to the leaders of both fleet groups that Qoribu's [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Kr]] was the site of the [[Kr nest|nest]] of the [[Gorog]] Kiliiks—who wanted the Dark Nest Crisis to escalate into further violence—and that [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and his [[Mara Jade Skywalker|wife]] were currently [[Battle of Kr|fighting]] them there. This prompted the Chiss and the Hapan fleets to [[Battle of Qoribu|attack]] one another after both tried to aid the Jedi and refused to back down. However, after the Gorog were defeated and the misunderstandings between the Chiss and the Hapans [[Qoribu Truce|sorted out]], the Solos devised a plan to move the Killiks from Qoribu to another [[Utegetu Nebula/Legends|system]] not under Chiss control in order to reduce tensions between the two [[Species/Legends|species]]. To achieve this, the Fleet of the Glorious Defender Queen departed the Gyuel system, [[Transport/Legends|transporting]] the Killiks in the holds of the Hapan vessels, with the ''Kendall'' carrying the [[Taat]] Killik nest itself. ===Flagship of the Heritage Fleet=== {{Quote|It's a long story, given our comm restrictions. Perhaps I could fill you in once I'm aboard?''"
"''You won't be aboard. The Heritage Fleet is preparing to make the attack-jump. Fall in at the back of the formation. You can explain after the battle.|Lalu Morwan requests permission to board the ''Kendall'' from Ducha AlGray|}} By [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], during the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], when the Galactic Alliance was fighting the planet [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia's]] [[Five Worlds]] government, the ''Kendall'' was still in Hapan service. That year, [[Ducha]] [[AlGray]] of House AlGray and the [[Heritage Council]] of Hapan [[Noble/Legends|nobles]] conspired with the Five Worlds to overthrow [[Gender/Legends|Queen Mother]] Djo in order to keep the Hapan [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]] independent of the Galactic Alliance. As the new flagship of the council's [[Heritage Fleet]], the ''Kendall'' staged with the rest of the fleet and three [[Corellian Dreadnaught]]s at a [[Comet/Legends|comet]] in Hapan space, where they arrayed themselves in a three-dimensional diamond formation to attack the planetary defenses of Hapes. Just before the ''Kendall'' and the Heritage Fleet departed for their attack, the ''Millennium Falcon'', once again under the alias ''Longshot'', found the fleet—[[Lalu Morwan]], a Hapan [[operative]] for the Heritage Council, was aboard the ''Millennium Falcon'' in addition to the Solos, who supported Corellia in the struggle despite members of their family and [[Skywalker family/Legends|relatives]] serving the Galactic Alliance. Morwan wanted to board the Battle Dragon, but AlGray, in command of the ''Kendall'', did not allow her to do so due to time constraints, and the battle group departed for Hapes with the ''Millennium Falcon'' tagging along. However, the Solos betrayed the reversion coordinates of the Heritage Fleet to Djo, and when the ''Kendall'' and the fleet exited hyperspace at Hapes, they were immediately set upon by Hapan forces loyal to the Queen Mother. The Heritage Fleet was badly damaged in the surprise attack, and the ''Kendall'' soon had a quarter of the Hapan Royal Navy firing upon it. In the end, the Hapan loyalists defeated the Heritage Council and their [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] allies with the help of the Galactic Alliance. ==Commanders and crew== {{Quote|You were at Qoribu? Aboard the ''Kendall''?''"
"''As a matter of fact, I did serve aboard the ''Kendall''.|Leia Organa and Lalu Morwan|}} At the time of the Battle of Qoribu, the ''Kendall'' was commanded by Dukat Aleson Gray, and among the ship's crew, Lalu Morwan served as a field [[Doctor/Legends|surgeon]]. By 40 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], the ''Kendall''{{'s}} command had been taken over by Ducha AlGray, who attempted a coup with the Heritage Council against Queen Mother Djo. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Kendall'' first appeared in [[2005]]'s [[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King|''The Joiner King'']], the lead novel in ''[[Star Wars: The Dark Nest Trilogy]]'', written by [[Troy Denning]]. The Battle Dragon later appeared in Denning's [[Legacy of the Force: Tempest|''Tempest'']], a [[2006]] installment of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' series. The ''Kendall'' was also mentioned in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. Although ''Tempest'' has the ''Kendall'' being attacked by a quarter of the Hapan Royal Navy during the Battle of Hapes, the story does not reveal the Battle Dragon's fate. ==Appearances== *''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Legacy of the Force: Tempest]]'' ==Sources== *{{CSWECite|I||Gray, Aleson}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Kendall |es=Kendall }} [[Category:Flagships]] [[Category:Hapan Battle Dragons]]
